A2.definition: A round fruit that is red, green, or yellow
A2.example: I eat an apple every morning.

A2.definition: Attractive and pleasant to look at

A2.example: My cat is sleeping on the sofa.

A2.definition: Feeling or showing pleasure or contentment
A2.example: She was very happy about the news.

A2.definition: A colorless, odorless, and tasteless liquid essential for life
A place where children go to learn
A2.example: I go to school every day.

A2.definition: A person you know well and like
Moving or capable of moving at high speed

B1.definition: To complete successfully or achieve a goal
B1.example: She accomplished her dream of becoming a doctor.
His benevolent nature earned him many friends.

B1.definition: To honor or remember an important event or person
B1.example: We commemorated the historical event with a ceremony.

B1.definition: Showing care and effort in your work
B1.example: She is a diligent student who always does her homework.
The wedding dress had an elaborate design with many details.

B2.definition: Open to more than one interpretation; unclear
B2.example: The contract contained ambiguous language that needed clarification.

B2.definition: Lasting for a very short time
B2.example: The beauty of cherry blossoms is ephemeral.

B2.definition: Showing great attention to detail; very careful and precise
B2.example: The architect was meticulous in planning the building.

B2.definition: Dealing with things in a practical, realistic way
B2.example: We need a pragmatic approach to solve this problem.

C1.definition: To deliberately make something unclear or difficult to understand
C1.example: The politician tried to obfuscate the issue during the debate.
Her perspicacious analysis of the market trends was impressive.

C1.definition: Occurring by chance in a happy or beneficial way
C1.example: Meeting him at the conference was a serendipitous encounter.
Smartphones are ubiquitous in modern society.

IELTS_5.example: The patient's condition began to deteriorate.

IELTS_5.definition: To make something less severe or serious
IELTS_5.example: Exercise can mitigate the effects of stress.

IELTS_6.definition: Lasting only for a short time; temporary
IELTS_6.example: The economic boom proved to be transient.

IELTS_6.definition: Logical and consistent; easy to understand
IELTS_6.example: His argument was coherent and well-structured.

IELTS_7.definition: To make a problem worse or more serious
Not in harmony or keeping with the surroundings
IELTS_7.example: His casual attitude was incongruous with the formal setting.
